top_stories.title: Attackers obtained encrypted password vaults from some Dashlane user accounts
top_stories.summary: Dashlane disclosed that a brute-force attack allowed threat actors to access some customer accounts and copy encrypted password vaults. The company stated no internal systems were compromised, with the incident first acknowledged on May 31 after users reported account suspensions and login issues.

top_stories.title: Microsoft 365 Android Apps Let Any App Steal Account Tokens via Leftover Debug Flag
top_stories.summary: A leftover debug flag in production builds of multiple Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled token-sharing restrictions, allowing any app on the same device to silently steal account tokens without authentication. The flaw permitted unauthorized access to email, files, calendar, and messaging as the authenticated user, bypassing all login and permission prompts.

top_stories.title: Coding Gaffe Exposes Microsoft 365 Accounts to Widespread Takeover
top_stories.summary: A coding error in Microsoft 365 Android apps disabled a security setting designed to protect authentication, exposing accounts to potential takeover. The vulnerability affected key applications including Word, PowerPoint, and Excel, allowing attackers to steal login credentials and data.

top_stories.title: FBI-Flagged Phishing Kit Kali365 Expands Its Reach
top_stories.summary: The phishing-as-a-service platform Kali365, previously flagged by the FBI for targeting Microsoft 365, has expanded its attack scope to include AWS, Okta, and Russian platforms. The platform employs device code phishing techniques to compromise identity and access credentials across these services.

top_stories.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
top_stories.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords and seize accounts, including those of the Obama White House and a U.S. Space Force official. Instructions for manipulating the AI chatbot into bypassing normal authentication and account recovery controls circulated on Telegram prior to the attacks.

news.title: Let’s Encrypt works toward post-quantum certificates at web scale
news.summary: Let's Encrypt is developing post-quantum-safe Web PKI using Merkle Tree Certificates (MTCs), targeting a staging environment in late 2026 and production readiness in 2027. The initiative focuses on adding post-quantum authentication to TLS without degrading the speed and reliability of certificate issuance at web scale.

news.title: Building secure B2C applications with fine-grained access control using Amazon Cognito and Amazon Verified Permissions
news.summary: Amazon has published guidance on building secure B2C applications using Amazon Cognito for authentication combined with Amazon Verified Permissions for fine-grained access control. The article covers implementing authentication and authorization controls together, addressing both identity verification and resource access management in modern web applications.

news.title: Willow raises $7M to build the identity layer for autonomous AI agents
news.summary: Willow has raised $7M to develop an identity layer specifically designed for autonomous AI agents. The funding targets a specialized identity management solution for agentic AI systems operating within enterprise environments.

news.title: The AWS MCP Server now supports cross-account and cross-role access
news.summary: AWS has added cross-account and cross-role access support to the AWS Model Context Protocol (MCP) Server within its Agent Toolkit. The feature enables developers using AI coding agents to operate across multiple AWS accounts and roles without manually switching credentials.

news.title: Implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect in ASP.NET Core
news.summary: An article provides guidance on implementing OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect protocols within ASP.NET Core applications. The content covers authentication, authorization, token management, and Single Sign-On configuration for developers building secure applications.

news.title: Customize federated sign-in with new Amazon Cognito Lambda trigger
news.summary: Amazon Cognito has introduced a new Lambda trigger that allows developers to customize federated sign-in flows in user pools. The feature enables customization of authentication behavior for users signing in via external identity providers within Cognito's managed identity service.

news.title: Gartner: Cyber Leaders Must Treat Identity as Infrastructure as AI Attack Surfaces Multiply
news.summary: Gartner has issued guidance stating that cyber leaders must treat identity as core infrastructure in response to expanding AI-driven attack surfaces. The advisory emphasizes modernizing identity systems and measuring resilience as key strategic priorities for security leaders.

news.title: Microsoft Fixes One-Click GitHub Dev Attack That Let Attackers Steal OAuth Tokens
news.summary: A security researcher disclosed a one-click attack in Microsoft Visual Studio Code that could steal GitHub OAuth tokens, allowing attackers to read and write to private repositories. The vulnerability involved the GitHub.dev feature and has since been patched by Microsoft.

news.title: Shrinking the IAM Attack Surface through Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP)
news.summary: The article addresses the concept of "Identity Dark Matter" in enterprise IAM environments, where identity activity occurs outside centralized visibility and control. It focuses on Identity Visibility and Intelligence Platforms (IVIP) as a solution to fragmented identity sprawl across applications, machine identities, and decentralized systems.

news.title: Understanding Microsoft Entra ID Tenant Object Quota
news.summary: This article explains Microsoft Entra ID's tenant object quota system, covering what it is and how to manage it. The content addresses capacity limits within Microsoft's identity platform for managing directory objects.

news.title: Amazon OpenSearch Service: Mechanisms to secure your domain
news.summary: Amazon published an overview of security mechanisms available for Amazon OpenSearch Service, covering authentication, authorization, encryption, and network access controls. The post details fine-grained access control implementation and AWS Identity and Access Management (IAM) integration for OpenSearch domain management.

news.title: Microsoft's Zero-Day Legal Threats Spark Backlash
news.summary: A security researcher published multiple zero-day exploits, prompting Microsoft to suggest potential criminal charges. The dispute centers on Microsoft's response to unsolicited vulnerability disclosures and has drawn backlash from the security research community.

news.title: With Complex Cloud Integrations, Small Errors Lead to Major Compromises
news.summary: Security researchers discovered an exploit chain in a popular automation service that combined over-permissioned roles, secrets discovery, and non-human identities to enable potential full compromise. The attack vector highlights risks associated with excessive IAM permissions and non-human identity (NHI) management in complex cloud integrations.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both congressional chambers are demanding answers while CISA works to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant privileged credential exposure incident.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in cryptocurrency theft from investors.
